title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation · Language Clinic: Incremental Merge
summary: A counting loop resets existing entries, aliases a shared dictionary, includes empty tokens, sorts while mutating, and never reconciles token totals with frequency totals. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the incremental merge increment in inspectable frequency engine. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Merge partial counters associatively and test partition independence for streaming and parallel transfer.
Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-incremental-merge — Merge partial counters associatively and test partition independence for streaming and parallel transfer.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-frequency-counter-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_frequency_counter_3(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-incremental-merge-dqs3xz
# Competency: bookbot-incremental-merge.
# Case 3346: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_incremental_merge_dqs3xz(token_count: int = 12, frequency_total: int = 12, vocabulary: int = 5, negative_counts: int = 0, owned_result: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dqs3xz = "dqs3xz-zx3sqd-3346"
    assert evidence_variant_dqs3xz
    evidence_axes_dqs3xz = (
        "corpusdqs3xz",
        "policyzx3sqd",
        "revision3346",
        "commanddqs3xz3346",
        "reportzx3sqd3346",
        "test3346dqs3xz",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dqs3xz)) == 6
    if token_count < 0 or vocabulary < 0:
        return False, token_count, "count-shape"
    if negative_counts > 0:
        return False, negative_counts, "negative-frequency"
    if frequency_total != token_count:
        return False, frequency_total, "reconciliation"
    if not owned_result:
        return False, vocabulary, "aliased-result"
    assert vocabulary <= token_count
    return True, vocabulary, "reconciled-counter"

Build deterministic Python evidence to produce an owned frequency map whose nonnegative counts reconcile to accepted tokens; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Merge partial counters associatively and test partition independence for streaming and parallel transfer.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Incremental Merge model · Theory Incremental Merge, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Adding independently normalized counters is safe even when their token policies differ.
